label: Brighthive 3.0
enforcement: status_board_only — this file is NOT wired into any deploy pipeline and does not block anything today. It has no admin/webhook relationship to the 5 app repos' branch protection. Revisit turning it into a real gate once BH-1145 (brighthive-e2e --gate wiring) ships — that's the natural place to add a manifest-status check, rather than building a second gate mechanism in parallel.
note: Umbrella release label, not a repo version. Each app repo keeps its own independent version field/tag scheme (see per-repo package.json/pyproject.toml + the vX.Y.Z.W-pre-release GitHub tag mechanism, both unchanged by this label). This file is the single source of truth mapping the '3.0' marketing/release number to the per-repo version each app was bumped to.

brightbot.status: done — 3.0.0 on both develop and main

brighthive-platform-core.status: blocked — 3.0.0 on develop, main PR #1121 needs 1 human approval (enforce_admins is ON for this repo's main branch — cannot be bypassed with --admin, unlike the other 4 repos)

brighthive-webapp.note_branch_naming: this repo has NO 'main' branch at all — its prod branch is named 'production'. develop/staging/production, not develop/staging/main.
brighthive-webapp.status: done — 3.0.0 on both develop and production

brightbot-slack-server.status: done — 3.0.0 on main (this repo's version-bump PR targeted main directly, not develop)

brighthive-admin.version_field: setuptools_scm (derived from git tags, no static field)
brighthive-admin.note: No git tags exist in this repo — setuptools_scm has nothing to derive a version from. Needs a v3.0.0 tag cut (BH-1144).

deploy_mechanism_unaffected: vX.Y.Z.W-pre-release GitHub release tags on platform-core/webapp continue to drive deploy-staging.yml/deploy-production.yml exactly as before — this manifest sits on top as a label, never as a new tag format.

ci_cd_gate_audit_2026_07_21.summary: None of the 5 app repos has a real staging→main promote gate today (unit+integration+e2e+env-var-health). Tracked as follow-up under epic BH-170.

tickets.brightbot: BH-1140 — CI unit-test gate disabled (if: false)
tickets.brighthive-platform-core: BH-1141 — no integration/e2e gate, env-var Zod schema never validated in CI
tickets.brighthive-webapp: BH-1142 — deploy.yml 'test' job runs zero tests
tickets.brightbot-slack-server: BH-1143 — no test step before deploy (validate-deploy-config.mjs env-health check is the good pattern to copy)
tickets.brighthive-e2e: BH-1145 — health-check.yml has a real --gate mode but is wired to zero repos

operational_learnings_2026_07_21.develop_main_divergence: A PR merged to develop does NOT mean it reaches main by itself. Confirmed 3 times this release: BH-1078 (PII masking), BH-776, BH-777 all merged to develop weeks earlier and were absent from main until hand-ported separately. main and develop had diverged enough in file structure that a straight cherry-pick of the develop squash-merge commit conflicted — several files it touched didn't exist on main at all. Each fix needed its own PR opened directly against main, re-verified there, not assumed from develop's state.
operational_learnings_2026_07_21.branch_naming_is_not_uniform: brighthive-webapp has no 'main' branch — its prod branch is 'production'. Check `git branch -r` per repo before assuming develop/staging/main everywhere.
operational_learnings_2026_07_21.enforce_admins_varies_per_repo: brighthive-platform-core has enforce_admins ON for its main branch — even a repo admin cannot bypass required review with `gh pr merge --admin`. The other 4 repos (brightbot, brighthive-webapp, brightbot-slack-server) allow the admin override. Check `gh api repos/{org}/{repo}/branches/main/protection` before assuming --admin will work uniformly across repos.
operational_learnings_2026_07_21.action_item: Add a real develop→main sync check to CI (BH-1145 is the natural home) — this class of drift went undetected for 4+ weeks on a P0 security fix with no automated signal.
